steps.title: Identify the defining characterization
steps.content: The phrase “This kind of hidden but still conscious glory suited the nature of the man” is the decisive clue. It reveals Tom Towers values quiet, private satisfaction—power and influence he possesses but doesn't openly flaunt.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content:
Choice B says he “takes private satisfaction in his capacity to influence public opinion.” This matches perfectly—the passage shows him loving to sit silently while thinking how politicians are “in his power,” which is exactly private satisfaction in quiet influence.

The other three either overstate his need for visible acclaim (A), misread his focus regarding provincial readers (C), or invert the relationship by making readers central to his satisfaction (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
